      • width(r: Rect): float32

Geometry primitives used across layout, painting and gestures. Mirrors Flutter's dart:ui geometry types: Offset, Size, Rect, Radius, RRect, EdgeInsets, Alignment, Constraints, plus the axis / alignment enums consumed by Row, Column, Stack.

All values are float32 logical pixels. None of these procs have side effects: they return new values rather than mutating inputs.

Types

Alignment = object
  x*, y*: float32
A 2D alignment in unit space. Each axis runs from -1.0 (start / top / left) through 0 (center) to +1.0 (end / bottom / right). Use the predefined alignCenter, alignTopLeft etc., or build one via alignment(x, y). 
Axis = enum
  axHorizontal, axVertical
Axis selector used by flex containers and scroll views. 
Constraints = object
  minWidth*, maxWidth*, minHeight*, maxHeight*: float32
Min/max bounds on the size a child may take. A child receives constraints from its parent during layout, then returns a Size that must satisfy minWidth <= size.width <= maxWidth and similarly for height. minWidth == maxWidth means the width is "tight" - the child must be exactly that wide. 
CrossAxisAlignment = enum
  caStart, caEnd, caCenter, caStretch, caBaseline
How children are placed along the cross axis. caStart/caEnd/caCenter align to that edge. caStretch forces each child to fill the cross-axis extent. caBaseline aligns text baselines (treated as caCenter here). 
EdgeInsets = object
  left*, top*, right*, bottom*: float32
Insets on each side of a rectangle, in logical pixels. Used for padding, margins, and constraint deflation. 
MainAxisAlignment = enum
  maStart, maEnd, maCenter, maSpaceBetween, maSpaceAround, maSpaceEvenly
How children are placed along a flex container's main axis. maStart packs to the start. maEnd packs to the end. maCenter centers them as a group. maSpaceBetween puts equal gaps only BETWEEN children. maSpaceAround puts half-gaps at the ends. maSpaceEvenly puts equal gaps everywhere including the ends. 
MainAxisSize = enum
  msMin, msMax
Whether a flex container shrinks to its children (msMin) or expands to its parent's max constraint (msMax). 
Offset = object
  dx*, dy*: float32
A 2D point or vector. dx is horizontal, dy is vertical (positive = down, matching screen coordinates). 
Radius = object
  x*, y*: float32
A possibly-elliptical corner radius. x is the horizontal radius, y the vertical. Use radius(r) for circular corners. 
Rect = object
  left*, top*, right*, bottom*: float32
An axis-aligned rectangle expressed by its four edges (not by origin + size). right >= left and bottom >= top when the rect is normalized. 
RRect = object
  rect*: Rect
  tl*, tr*, bl*, br*: Radius
A rectangle with four corner radii. Use rrect(rect, all) to build one with uniform corners. 
Size = object
  width*, height*: float32
A 2D size, always non-negative by convention. width and height are in logical pixels. 
TextDirection = enum
  tdLtr, tdRtl
Text directionality. tdLtr for left-to-right, tdRtl for right-to-left. 
VerticalDirection = enum
  vdUp, vdDown
Direction in which a column lays out its children.

Consts

alignBottomCenter = (x: 0.0, y: 1.0)
Bottom-center anchor. 
alignBottomLeft = (x: -1.0, y: 1.0)
Bottom-left anchor. 
alignBottomRight = (x: 1.0, y: 1.0)
Bottom-right anchor. 
alignCenter = (x: 0.0, y: 0.0)
Centered both axes. 
alignCenterLeft = (x: -1.0, y: 0.0)
Vertically centered, left anchor. 
alignCenterRight = (x: 1.0, y: 0.0)
Vertically centered, right anchor. 
alignTopCenter = (x: 0.0, y: -1.0)
Top-center anchor. 
alignTopLeft = (x: -1.0, y: -1.0)
Top-left anchor. 
alignTopRight = (x: 1.0, y: -1.0)
Top-right anchor. 
OffsetZero = (dx: 0.0, dy: 0.0)
The zero offset (origin). 
RectZero = (left: 0.0, top: 0.0, right: 0.0, bottom: 0.0)
The empty rect at the origin. 
SizeZero = (width: 0.0, height: 0.0)
The zero size.

Procs

proc `$`(c: Constraints): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Debug string representation showing both ranges. 
proc `$`(o: Offset): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Debug string representation. 
proc `$`(r: Rect): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Debug string representation. 
proc `$`(s: Size): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Debug string representation. 
proc `*`(a: Offset; s: float32): Offset {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Scalar multiplication. 
proc `+`(a, b: Offset): Offset {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Vector addition. 
proc `-`(a, b: Offset): Offset {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Vector subtraction. 
proc alignment(x, y: float32): Alignment {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Builds an Alignment from explicit components in [-1, +1]. 
proc area(s: Size): float32 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns width * height. 
proc bottomRight(r: Rect): Offset {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
The bottom-right corner as an Offset. 
proc center(r: Rect): Offset {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
The center point of the rectangle. 
proc constrain(c: Constraints; s: Size): Size {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Clamps s to the c bounds component-wise. Useful in performLayout to compute the final size after measuring a child. 
proc constraints(minW, maxW, minH, maxH: float32): Constraints {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Builds a Constraints from explicit bounds. Caller is responsible for ensuring minW <= maxW and minH <= maxH. 
proc contains(r: Rect; p: Offset): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
True when p lies inside r. The left/top edges are inclusive, the right/bottom edges are exclusive (matching half-open hit-testing conventions). 
proc deflate(c: Constraints; e: EdgeInsets): Constraints {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Shrinks both max and min by the corresponding e totals (e.horizontal, e.vertical). All results are clamped at zero. Used by Padding to compute the constraints passed to its child. 
proc deflate(r: Rect; d: float32): Rect {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Shrinks r inward by d on every side. Negative d grows. 
proc deflateRect(e: EdgeInsets; r: Rect): Rect {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Shrinks r inward by e on each side. 
proc deflateSize(e: EdgeInsets; s: Size): Size {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Subtracts the insets from a size, clamping at zero so the result is never negative. 
proc distance(a: Offset): float32 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Euclidean magnitude sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy). 
proc edgeInsetsAll(v: float32): EdgeInsets {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Equal insets on every side. Equivalent to Flutter's EdgeInsets.all(v). 
proc edgeInsetsLTRB(l, t, r, b: float32): EdgeInsets {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
All four sides specified positionally in LTRB order. 
proc edgeInsetsOnly(left = 0.0'f32; top = 0.0'f32; right = 0.0'f32;
                    bottom = 0.0'f32): EdgeInsets {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Per-side insets, defaulting to zero on the sides you don't pass. 
proc edgeInsetsSymmetric(horizontal, vertical: float32): EdgeInsets {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Same inset on the left and right (horizontal) and on the top and bottom (vertical). 
proc enforce(c, parent: Constraints): Constraints {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Clamps each bound of c to lie within parent's bounds. Equivalent to Flutter's BoxConstraints.enforce(parent). Use this when a widget wants to apply additional constraints on top of what its parent already gave it. 
proc hasBoundedHeight(c: Constraints): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
True if maxHeight is finite. 
proc hasBoundedWidth(c: Constraints): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
True if maxWidth is finite. 
proc hash(o: Offset): Hash {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Hash combining both components. Lets Offset be used as a Table key. 
proc height(r: Rect): float32 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
bottom - top. 
proc horizontal(e: EdgeInsets): float32 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Total horizontal inset (left + right). 
proc inflate(r: Rect; d: float32): Rect {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Grows r outward by d on every side. 
proc intersect(a, b: Rect): Rect {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns the intersection of a and b. May be empty (right < left or bottom < top) if the rectangles don't overlap. 
proc isFinite(s: Size): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
True when neither dimension is Inf. 
proc isTight(c: Constraints): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
True when both axes are tight (min == max). 
proc lerp(a, b: EdgeInsets; t: float32): EdgeInsets {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Linear interpolation between two EdgeInsets (per-side). 
proc lerp(a, b: Offset; t: float32): Offset {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Linear interpolation between a and b component-wise. t = 0 returns a, t = 1 returns b. Values outside [0, 1] extrapolate. 
proc lerp(a, b: Size; t: float32): Size {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Linear interpolation between two sizes. 
proc looseFor(s: Size): Constraints {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Loose constraints with min 0 and max equal to s's dimensions. The child may be any size from zero up to s. 
proc loosen(c: Constraints): Constraints {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns a copy of c with both min set to zero. Caller can pass any size from 0 up to the original max. 
proc offset(dx, dy: float32): Offset {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Builds an Offset from two components. 
proc radius(r: float32): Radius {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Builds a circular Radius of r pixels. 
proc radiusElliptical(x, y: float32): Radius {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Builds an elliptical Radius with independent horizontal and vertical components. 
proc rect(left, top, right, bottom: float32): Rect {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Builds a Rect from its four edges. 
proc rectFromLTWH(l, t, w, h: float32): Rect {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Builds a Rect from origin + size. 
proc rectFromOffsetSize(o: Offset; s: Size): Rect {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Builds a Rect from an Offset (top-left) and a Size. 
proc resolveOffset(a: Alignment; parent, child: Size): Offset {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns the offset where child should be placed inside parent to satisfy alignment a. For example with alignCenter the result is ((parent.width - child.width)/2, (parent.height - child.height)/2). 
proc rrect(r: Rect; all: float32): RRect {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Builds an RRect (rounded rectangle) with uniform corner radius all. 
proc shift(r: Rect; o: Offset): Rect {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Translates r by o. 
proc size(w, h: float32): Size {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Builds a Size from explicit width and height. 
proc tightFor(s: Size): Constraints {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Convenience overload taking a Size. 
proc tightFor(w, h: float32): Constraints {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Builds tight constraints: minWidth == maxWidth == w and similarly for h. The child MUST take exactly that size. 
proc topLeft(r: Rect): Offset {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
The top-left corner as an Offset. 
proc topLeftOffset(e: EdgeInsets): Offset {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
The (left, top) corner as an Offset - used to position a child inside its padding container. 
proc unbounded(): Constraints {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Loose constraints with infinite max on both axes. Use sparingly: children with no fixed size will not know how big to be. 
proc vertical(e: EdgeInsets): float32 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Total vertical inset (top + bottom). 
proc width(r: Rect): float32 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
right - left.

Made with Nim. Generated: 2026-06-09 05:33:48 UTC
